name: Poverty Status
description: This layer shows poverty status by age group. This is shown by tract, county, and state boundaries. This service is updated annually to contain the most currently released American Community Survey (ACS) 5-year data, and contains estimates and margins of error. There are also additional calculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis. Poverty status is based on income in past 12 months of survey. <div><br /></div><div>This layer is symbolized to show the percentage of the population whose income falls below the Federal poverty line. To see the full list of attributes available in this service, go to the &quot;Data&quot; tab, and choose &quot;Fields&quot; at the top right. <div><br /></div><div><div><b>Current Vintage</b>: <span style="font-family:&quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size:16px;">2019-2023</span></div><div><b>ACS Table(s)</b>: B17020, C17002</div><div><b>Data downloaded from</b>: <a>Census Bureau's API for American Community Survey</a> </div><div><b><br /></b></div><div><b>The United States Census Bureau's American Community Survey (ACS):</b></div><div><ul><li><a>About the Survey</a><br /></li><li><a>Geography &amp; ACS</a><br /></li><li><a>Technical Documentation</a><br /></li><li><a>News &amp; Updates</a><br /></li></ul><div>This ready-to-use layer can be used within ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, its configurable apps, dashboards, Story Maps, custom apps, and mobile apps. Data can also be exported for offline workflows. For more information about ACS layers, visit the <a>FAQ</a>. Please cite the Census and ACS when using this data.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div><b>Data Note from the Census:</b><br /></div><div>Data are based on a sample and are subject to sampling variability. The degree of uncertainty for an estimate arising from sampling variability is represented through the use of a margin of error. The value shown here is the 90 percent margin of error. The margin of error can be interpreted as providing a 90 percent probability that the interval defined by the estimate minus the margin of error and the estimate plus the margin of error (the lower and upper confidence bounds) contains the true value. In addition to sampling variability, the ACS estimates are subject to nonsampling error (for a discussion of nonsampling variability, see <a>Accuracy of the Data</a>).  The effect of nonsampling error is not represented in these tables.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><b>Data Processing Notes:</b></div><div><ul><li>This layer is updated automatically when the most current vintage of ACS data is released each year, usually in December. The layer always contains the latest available ACS 5-year estimates. It is updated annually within days of the Census Bureau's release schedule. <a>Click here</a> to learn more about ACS data releases.<br /></li><li>Boundaries come from the <a>US Census TIGER geodatabases,</a> specifically, the National Sub-State Geography Database (named tlgdb_(year)_a_us_substategeo.gdb). Boundaries are updated at the same time as the data updates (annually), and the boundary vintage appropriately matches the data vintage <a>as specified by the Census</a>. These are Census boundaries with water and/or coastlines erased for cartographic and mapping purposes. For census tracts, the water cutouts are derived from a subset of the <a>2020 Areal Hydrography boundaries offered by TIGER</a>. Water bodies and rivers which are 50 million square meters or larger (mid to large sized water bodies) are erased from the tract level boundaries, as well as additional important features. For state and county boundaries, the water and coastlines are derived from the coastlines of the 2022 500k <a>TIGER Cartographic Boundary Shapefiles</a>. These are erased to more accurately portray the coastlines and Great Lakes. The original AWATER and ALAND fields are still available as attributes within the data table (units are square meters).</li><li>The States layer contains 52 records - all US states, Washington D.C., and Puerto Rico</li><li>Census tracts with no population that occur in areas of water, such as oceans, are removed from this data service (Census Tracts beginning with 99).</li><li>Percentages and derived counts, and associated margins of error, are calculated values (that can be identified by the &quot;_calc_&quot; stub in the field name), and abide by the specifications <a>defined by the American Community Survey</a>.</li><li>Field alias names were created based on the Table Shells file available from the <a>American Community Survey Summary File Documentation page</a>.<br /></li><li>Negative values (e.g., -4444...) have been set to null, with the exception of -5555... which has been set to zero. These negative values exist in the raw API data to indicate the following situations:</li><ul><li>The margin of error column indicates that either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute a standard error and thus the margin of error. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>Either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute an estimate, or a ratio of medians cannot be calculated because one or both of the median estimates falls in the lowest interval or upper interval of an open-ended distribution.<br /></li><li>The median falls in the lowest interval of an open-ended distribution, or in the upper interval of an open-ended distribution. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>The estimate is controlled. A statistical test for sampling variability is not appropriate.<br /></li><li>The data for this geographic area cannot be displayed because the number of sample cases is too small.</li></ul></ul></div></div></div>

fields.name: OBJECTID
fields.alias: Object ID
fields.description: {"value":"An ObjectID is a unique, not null integer field used to uniquely identify rows in tables in a geographical table.", "fieldValueType":"uniqueIdentifier"}

fields.name: Shape__Area
fields.alias: Shape__Area

fields.name: Shape__Length
fields.alias: Shape__Length

fields.name: GEOID
fields.alias: Geographic Identifier - FIPS Code
fields.description: {"value":"Geographic Identifier, or GEOID is the numeric code used to uniquely identify all administrative/legal and statistical geographic areas for which the Census Bureau tabulates data.", "fieldValueType":"uniqueIdentifier"}

fields.name: ALAND
fields.alias: Area of Land (Square Meters)
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of land in square meters within this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"measurement"}

fields.name: AWATER
fields.alias: Area of Water (Square Meters)
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of water in square meters within this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"measurement"}

fields.name: NAME
fields.alias: Name
fields.description: {"value":"Name of this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: State
fields.alias: State
fields.description: {"value":"State in which this geography resides", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: County
fields.alias: County
fields.description: {"value":"County in which this geography resides", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: B17020_001E
fields.alias: Total Population for whom Poverty Status is Determined
fields.description: {"value":"Total population for whom poverty status is determined. Poverty status was determined for all people except institutionalized people, people in military group quarters, people in college dormitories, and unrelated individuals under 15 years old. These groups were excluded from the numerator and denominator when calculating poverty rates.","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_001M
fields.alias: Total Population for whom Poverty Status is Determined - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for total population for whom poverty status is determined. Poverty status was determined for all people except institutionalized people, people in military group quarters, people in college dormitories, and unrelated individuals under 15 years old. These groups were excluded from the numerator and denominator when calculating poverty rates.","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_002E
fields.alias: Population whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level
fields.description: {"value":"Population whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income.","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_002M
fields.alias: Population whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for population whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level. fields.alias: Population whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level 75 to 84 years
fields.description: {"value":"Population 75 to 84 years whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income.","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_016M
fields.alias: Population whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level 75 to 84 years - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for population 75 to 84 years whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income.","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_017E
fields.alias: Population whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level 85 years and over
fields.description: {"value":"Population 85 years and over whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income.","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_017M
fields.alias: Population whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level 85 years and over - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for population 85 years and over whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income.","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_pctPovE
fields.alias: Percent of Population whose income in the past 12 months is below poverty level
fields.description: {"value":"Percent of population whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B17020_002E / B17020_001E , 1)","fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_pctPovM
fields.alias: Percent of Population whose income in the past 12 months is below poverty level - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for percent of population whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level (90% confidence). A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income. Esri calculated field: (100.0/B17020_001E)*(sqrt((B17020_002M)^2-1.0*((B17020_002E / B17020_001E)^2)*(B17020_001M)^2))","fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_numChildPovE
fields.alias: Child Population (under 18 years) whose income in the past 12 months is below poverty level
fields.description: {"value":"Population under 18 years whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income. Esri calculated field: B17020_003E + B17020_004E + B17020_005E","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_numChildPovM
fields.alias: Child Population (under 18 years) whose income in the past 12 months is below poverty level - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for population under 18 years whose income in the past 12 months is at or above federal poverty level (90% confidence). A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income. Esri calculated field: sqrt(B17020_003M^2 + B17020_004M^2 + B17020_005M^2)","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_pctChildPovE
fields.alias: Percent of Children (under 18 years) whose income in the past 12 months is below poverty level
fields.description: {"value":"Percent of population under 18 whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level. A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B17020_calc_numChildPovE / B17020_calc_numTotChildE, 1)","fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_pctChildPovM
fields.alias: Percent of Children (under 18 years) whose income in the past 12 months is below poverty level - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for percent of population under 18 years whose income in the past 12 months is below federal poverty level (90% confidence). A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage/salary income; net self-employment income; interest/dividends/net rental/royalty income/income from estates & trusts; Social Security/Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance/welfare payments; retirement/survivor/disability pensions; & all other income. Esri calculated field: (100.0/B17020_calc_numTotChildE)*(sqrt((B17020_calc_numChildPovM)^2-1.0*((B17020_calc_numChildPovE / B17020_calc_numTotChildE)^2)*(B17020_calc_numTotChildM)^2))","fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_numTotChildE
fields.alias: Child Population (under 18 years) for whom Poverty Status is Determined
fields.description: {"value":"Total population under 18 years for whom poverty status is determined. Poverty status was determined for all people except institutionalized people, people in military group quarters, people in college dormitories, and unrelated individuals under 15 years old. These groups were excluded from the numerator and denominator when calculating poverty rates. Esri calculated field: B17020_003E + B17020_004E + B17020_005E + B17020_011E + B17020_012E + B17020_013E","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B17020_calc_numTotChildM
fields.alias: Child Population (under 18 years) for whom Poverty Status is Determined - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for total population under 18 years for whom poverty status is determined (90% confidence). Poverty status was determined for all people except institutionalized people, people in military group quarters, people in college dormitories, and unrelated individuals under 15 years old. These groups were excluded from the numerator and denominator when calculating poverty rates. Esri calculated field: sqrt(B17020_003M^2 +B17020_004M^2 + B17020_005M^2 + B17020_011M^2 + B17020_012M^2 + B17020_013M^2)","fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

A family and every individual in it are considered to be in poverty if the family's total income is less than the dollar value of a threshold that varies depending upon size of family, number of children, & age of householder (for 1- & 2- person households). Income is the sum of wage / salary income; net self-employment income; interest / dividends / net rental / royalty income / income from estates & trusts; Social Security / Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance / welfare payments; retirement / survivor / disability pensions; & all other income. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B17020_001E)*(sqrt((C17002_005M)^2-1.0*((C17002_005E / B17020_001E)^2)*(B17020_001M)^2))","fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}
